subject: Computer Networking
title: Edge Computing for Mobile
description: A fast breakdown of Edge Computing for Mobile for builders.

hook.text: A self-driving car can't wait 50 ms for a server response. Edge computing puts processing closer to the device.

buildup.text: Edge servers sit at cell towers or local hubs, just milliseconds away. Data doesn't need to travel to a distant cloud.

discovery.text: This slashes latency for real-time apps: AR overlays, gaming, industrial control, and autonomous vehicles.

twist.text: Edge doesn't replace the cloud — it extends it. Heavy processing stays central; time-critical tasks go to the edge.

climax.text: 5G and edge computing are designed to work together. Network slicing directs edge traffic with guaranteed latency.

punchline.text: Edge computing: cloud power, local speed.

quiz.question: What is the main benefit of edge computing?
options: Reducing latency by processing data closer to the device
Storing more data than a traditional cloud
Eliminating the need for internet connectivity
